ab: pakbalayq ti makitaal i kumnuriax casan qu?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: balayq ga, papkitaal ti inu’?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: makitalay ti papatasan qu? paagal cami cu qalimu’.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: nanu’ halay ti papasutuan ga, papsituwing ci cu situwing na Itaal i musa’.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: kehma i makpahpah ku qaym na kabahneq i tinqalangan niyam ga balayq cubaly i talan, iy makpahpah lga wahi ci kumiyum ga!
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: aqu asiki nagaun ruu’ gabagan ka tikay, ingasug cuqaang lqu!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: nanu’ uwah i tatubunan cu kinloh la!
ch: W: 那你豐年祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Harvest Festival!

ab: uwal la! paqasaw mi numaga’!
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: micuwa’ su’ ka mabuwinah lawwi maqiyanux i tinqalangan?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: mausa’ ci pasibaq cu ruwas.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: micuwa’ ka ini’ kakiya i tinqalangan quwis pasibaq cu ruwas pisa?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: aqeh gi lungulungun mu ku inkahulan mu, muwa’ na kahuwayun ti pasayug ku rngu’ ta’ mha cu uwi.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: baaq si cubalay i tuminun cu capang, ima’ ku pinasibaq isu’?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: yaki’ mu, ihya’ cubalay ku capaang na baaq i tuminun cu capang i tinqalangan niyam.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: micuwa’ su’ ka miki makibaq cu tatinun cu capang?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: Gaga’ niyam cubalay ku tuminun cu capang, paluhingaw ta’ ku Gaga’ mha ci lumanglung.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: balayq cubalay i nubuun ku quwaw na turakis ka hani.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: pinuquwaw mu nanak yo!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: baaq su’ cubalay! ingasug i puquwawun qu?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: bali baqun, ani ini’ kaanga’ ga tugal, imagal cu buwating, quga kaingasugun cuqaang i lumqing lawwi. ubugan, hupungun, basawan han ku mami’ na turakis. paqupaqun cu manbaxbux ku tamul, anabalabalayqun i umimag kinku mami’ na turakis ru tukuraun ca matapah, tuwangan lawwi cu masamag kinku mami’ na turakis ka tala’tu’ na tinahuk ka qusiya’ ru’ tarahrahgan i umumuk sumirhu la. ingasug sukuun ga ararruwa i masiukas ku mami’ na turakis ru payux uwi i buqan a quwaw ru’ sahahiya’ cubalabalay i nubuun. puquwaw na riax cu balayq na quwaw! kaa kugi i numaga’…!
ch: W:不一定，快則起碼三、五個月，甚或更久。將小米浸泡、蒸熟、冷卻後，將酒麴搗碎成粉末狀均灑於小米飯上並充分攪拌均勻，裝入罈甕裡，再淋上一定比例的冷開水，就可以封蓋存窖了。越久小米飯越溶解，酒液量也越多且越陳越香醇。讓時光釀出一罈美酒喽！耐心等待吧…
en: Depends on the situation, but it takes at least three to five months or even longer. After soaking, steaming, and cooling the millet, the koji is pounded into powdered form, sprinkled on top of the millet, stirred well, packed into urns, and poured with a certain percentage of cold boiled water, then sealed and stored in a cellar. The longer the rice is stored, the more it dissolves, the more fluid the sake becomes, and the better the flavor. Let time brew the wine! Wait patiently
